title_original: D-Wave, Anduril, Davidson to Collaborate on Quantum Applications for Defense - NAM
summary_original: D-Wave, Anduril Industries, and Davidson Technologies announced a collaboration to develop quantum-classical hybrid solutions for United States air and missile defense. Their initial proof of concept combined Anduril’s defense simulations, Davidson’s mission-domain modeling, and D-Wave’s quantum computing. In tests comparing classical and hybrid quantum approaches for missile-defense planning, D-Wave’s Stride hybrid solver was at least 10 times faster, improved threat mitigation by 9–12 percent, and intercepted 45–60 more missiles out of 500 in simulated attacks. According to D-Wave CEO Alan Baratz, this demonstrates that annealing quantum computing is already viable for mission-critical defense applications. The partnership plans to explore further use cases, including manufacturing and cyber defense.
title_fixed: D-Wave, Anduril, Davidson to Collaborate on Quantum Applications for Defense - NAM

In tests comparing classical and hybrid quantum approaches for missile-defense planning, D-Wave’s Stride hybrid solver was at least 10 times faster, improved threat mitigation by 9–12 percent, and intercepted 45–60 more missiles out of 500 in simulated attacks. According to D-Wave CEO Alan Baratz, this demonstrates that annealing quantum computing is already viable for mission-critical defense applications.
